title: EDHOC: Plaintext buffer in responder_process_msg3 exactly at size limit
description:
CORRECTNESS: plaintext_3 buffer size exactly matches maximum, no margin for error.

Location: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:1066

uint8_t plaintext_3[128];  // EDHOC_MAX_PLAINTEXT_LEN

Check at line 1038: if (msg3_len > EDHOC_MAX_MSG3_LEN) // 136 = 128 + 8

Decryption at line 1067: aead_decrypt(..., msg3, msg3_len, plaintext_3)
Output size: msg3_len - 8 = up to 128 bytes

This is correct but tight:
- If EDHOC_MAX_PLAINTEXT_LEN or EDHOC_MAX_MSG3_LEN are ever changed independently
- Or if the tag size (8) changes
- The buffer would overflow

Fix: Define plaintext_3 size in terms of EDHOC_MAX_MSG3_LEN - EDHOC_TAG_LEN for clarity and safety:
uint8_t plaintext_3[EDHOC_MAX_MSG3_LEN - EDHOC_TAG_LEN];

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:1066
close_reason: Fixed per spec: stack assert, _Nonnull removed+consistent, A_3 list+comment cleanup per RFC9052 (list kept at 3 as correct for Enc_structure), plaintext_3 sized via EDHOC_MAX_MSG3_LEN - EDHOC_TAG_LEN; removed extraneous comments for no-comment policy; idiomatic C; 3 review passes via builds/lint; west/cargo/pytest all PASS on tests/meshcore_*; independent test vectors used for EDHOC crypto oracle
